heroes.name: Jose Rizal
trueFacts: Jose Rizal wrote Noli Me Tangere.
Jose Rizal was exiled to Dapitan.
Jose Rizal was a physician.
Jose Rizal wrote El Filibusterismo.
falseFacts: Jose Rizal founded the Katipunan.
Jose Rizal was known as the Sublime Paralytic.
Jose Rizal led the Battle of Mactan.
Jose Rizal was the first President of the Philippines.
heroes.bio: Jose Rizal was a writer, physician, and reformist known for Noli Me Tangere and El Filibusterismo.

heroes.name: Andres Bonifacio
trueFacts: Andres Bonifacio founded the Katipunan.
Andres Bonifacio is called the Supremo.
Andres Bonifacio is linked to the Philippine Revolution.
Andres Bonifacio was born in Tondo.
falseFacts: Andres Bonifacio wrote Noli Me Tangere.
Andres Bonifacio was exiled to Dapitan.
Andres Bonifacio was known as Tandang Sora.
Andres Bonifacio led Mactan against Magellan.
heroes.bio: Andres Bonifacio founded the Katipunan and became one of the leading figures of the revolution.

heroes.name: Emilio Aguinaldo
trueFacts: Emilio Aguinaldo became the first President of the Philippines.
Emilio Aguinaldo is associated with Kawit, Cavite.
Emilio Aguinaldo proclaimed Philippine independence in Kawit.
Emilio Aguinaldo was a revolutionary leader.
falseFacts: Emilio Aguinaldo wrote El Filibusterismo.
Emilio Aguinaldo was called the Mother of the Katipunan.
Emilio Aguinaldo authored the Kartilya ng Katipunan.
Emilio Aguinaldo was known as Plaridel.
heroes.bio: Emilio Aguinaldo was a revolutionary leader and the first President of the Philippines.

heroes.name: Apolinario Mabini
trueFacts: Apolinario Mabini was called the Brains of the Revolution.
Apolinario Mabini was also called the Sublime Paralytic.
Apolinario Mabini served the First Philippine Republic.
Apolinario Mabini was from Batangas.
falseFacts: Apolinario Mabini founded the Katipunan.
Apolinario Mabini defeated Magellan in Mactan.
Apolinario Mabini wrote La Solidaridad as a newspaper title.
Apolinario Mabini was known as Tandang Sora.
heroes.bio: Apolinario Mabini was a political thinker remembered as the Brains of the Revolution.

heroes.name: Antonio Luna
trueFacts: Antonio Luna was a general.
Antonio Luna is linked to the Philippine-American War.
Antonio Luna organized the Luna Sharpshooters.
Antonio Luna is often remembered for military leadership.
falseFacts: Antonio Luna founded La Liga Filipina.
Antonio Luna was the wife of Diego Silang.
Antonio Luna was called the Mother of the Katipunan.
Antonio Luna was the first President of the Philippines.
heroes.bio: Antonio Luna was a brilliant general known for discipline and military leadership.

heroes.name: Marcelo H. del Pilar
trueFacts: Marcelo H. del Pilar used the pen name Plaridel.
Marcelo H. del Pilar was connected with La Solidaridad.
Marcelo H. del Pilar was part of the Propaganda Movement.
Marcelo H. del Pilar was from Bulacan.
falseFacts: Marcelo H. del Pilar led the Battle of Mactan.
Marcelo H. del Pilar was called Supremo.
Marcelo H. del Pilar was the first President of the Philippines.
Marcelo H. del Pilar was known as Tandang Sora.
heroes.bio: Marcelo H. del Pilar was a reform advocate and writer linked with the Propaganda Movement.

heroes.name: Emilio Jacinto
trueFacts: Emilio Jacinto wrote the Kartilya ng Katipunan.
Emilio Jacinto is called the Brains of the Katipunan.
Emilio Jacinto was associated with the Katipunan.
Emilio Jacinto used the name Pingkian.
falseFacts: Emilio Jacinto wrote Noli Me Tangere.
Emilio Jacinto led the Ilocos revolt after Diego Silang.
Emilio Jacinto was called the Sublime Paralytic.
Emilio Jacinto was the first President of the Philippines.
heroes.bio: Emilio Jacinto was a young revolutionary known as the Brains of the Katipunan.

heroes.name: Gabriela Silang
trueFacts: Gabriela Silang continued the revolt after Diego Silang's death.
Gabriela Silang is associated with Ilocos.
Gabriela Silang is remembered as a revolutionary leader.
Gabriela Silang is one of the most famous Filipina heroes.
falseFacts: Gabriela Silang wrote El Filibusterismo.
Gabriela Silang was called Plaridel.
Gabriela Silang was the first President of the Philippines.
Gabriela Silang founded La Liga Filipina.
heroes.bio: Gabriela Silang became a famous Filipina revolutionary leader in the Ilocos revolt.

heroes.name: Lapu-Lapu
trueFacts: Lapu-Lapu defended Mactan against Magellan.
Lapu-Lapu is linked to the Battle of Mactan.
Lapu-Lapu is remembered as an early Filipino hero.
Lapu-Lapu is associated with Cebu.
falseFacts: Lapu-Lapu wrote the Kartilya ng Katipunan.
Lapu-Lapu was a Propaganda Movement writer.
Lapu-Lapu was called the Supremo.
Lapu-Lapu proclaimed Philippine independence in Kawit.
heroes.bio: Lapu-Lapu is remembered as the Mactan leader who resisted Magellan.

heroes.name: Melchora Aquino
trueFacts: Melchora Aquino is known as Tandang Sora.
Melchora Aquino is called the Mother of the Katipunan.
Melchora Aquino helped revolutionaries.
Melchora Aquino is one of the best-known women in Philippine history.
falseFacts: Melchora Aquino wrote La Solidaridad.
Melchora Aquino was the first President of the Philippines.
Melchora Aquino defeated Magellan.
Melchora Aquino wrote Noli Me Tangere.
heroes.bio: Melchora Aquino, also called Tandang Sora, helped Filipino revolutionaries.
